elements.name: question1
elements.html:
Equity in CS Self-paced Online Professional Learning for Educators

Pre-Module Survey

Code.org relies on surveys as a crucial tool through which we remain connected to teachers, to give you the opportunity to provide feedback about our curriculum and your teaching experience, as well as to improve our programs.

Privacy and Confidentiality

  • Your individual responses here are completely confidential.
  • Your identity will never be revealed publicly or used to tie any responses back to you personally.
  • We may share anonymous, aggregate data (representing all responses) publicly.
  • Your anonymized responses will be used by Code.org for program improvement.

Your honesty and candor are appreciated.

Equity in CS - Pre-Module Survey

elements.html: The questions in this section aim to understand your experience with equity concepts and teaching strategies.

elements.title: Please share some of the strategies you currently use to create an inclusive CS classroom environment.

elements.name: section2_demographics
elements.html:
Teaching Context

The questions in this section aim to understand your role as an educator.

As a reminder this survey is confidential. We collect this information for program improvement. None of your responses will ever be tied back to you personally.

elements.name: teacher_role
elements.title: Which best describes your role as an educator?

choices.text: Out-of-school educator (e.g. tutor, after school teacher)

elements.description: (Please enter a number 0-90: enter 1 if this is your first year)

elements.title: How many years have you been teaching Computer Science at the K-12 level?
elements.description: (Please enter a number 0-90: enter 0 if you don't consider yourself a Computer Science teacher; enter 1 if this is your first year)

elements.title: At what grade levels did you teach this year?
